content.problemStatement:
Kefaa has developed a novel decomposition of a tree. He claims that this decomposition solves many difficult problems related to trees. However, he doesn't know how to find it quickly, so he asks you to help him.

You are given a tree with NNN vertices numbered 111 through NNN. Let's denote an edge between vertices uuu and vvv by (u,v)(u, v)(u,v). The triple-tree decomposition is a partition of edges of the tree into unordered triples of edges (a,b),(a,c),(a,d)(a, b), (a, c), (a, d)(a,b),(a,c),(a,d) such that a≠b≠c≠da \neq b \neq c \neq da=b=c=d. Each edge must belong to **exactly one** triple.

Help Kefaa with this problem — find a triple-tree decomposition of the given tree or determine that no such decomposition exists.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
2≤N≤50002 \le N \le 50002≤N≤5000 and the sum of NNN overall testcases doesn't exceed 500050005000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the following N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv describing an edge between vertices uuu and vvv of the tree.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a line containing the string `"YES"` if a triple-tree decomposition of the given tree exists or `"NO"` otherwise.
If it exists, print N−13\frac{N-1}{3}3N−1​ more lines describing a decomposition.
Each of these lines should contain four space-separated integers aaa, bbb, ccc and ddd describing a triple of edges (a,b),(a,c),(a,d)(a, b), (a, c), (a, d)(a,b),(a,c),(a,d).
If more than one triple-tree decomposition exists, you may output any one.
